action_ref.content:
{
  "analysis": "I can see there are files with 0600 permissions (private.key, db.conf) that need to be excluded, and symlinks that need to be handled. Now I need to check some text files for line endings and create the comprehensive build script.",
  "plan": "I'll check line endings in some text files, then create the build.sh script that handles all requirements: exclusions, permission normalization, line ending conversion, timestamp setting, and deterministic compression.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "file src/src/windows.py src/src/unix.py src/src/main.c 2>/dev/null
",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "hexdump -C src/src/windows.py | head -2
",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > build.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Deterministic build script for reproducible archives

SOURCE_DIR="/app/src"
OUTPUT_ARCHIVE="/app/release.tar.zst"
TEMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
WORK_DIR="$TEMP_DIR/work"

# Set default SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH if not set
SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH="${SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH:-946684800}"
export SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH

cleanup() {
    rm -rf "$TEMP_DIR"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

echo "Creating reproducible archive from $SOURCE_DIR"
echo "Using SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH: $SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH"

# Create work directory
mkdir -p "$WORK_DIR"

# Copy source tree with exclusions
echo "Copying source files with exclusions..."
rsync -a \
    --exclude='.git/' \
    --exclude='node_modules/' \
    --exclude='tmp/' \
    --exclude='.cache/' \
    --exclude='__pycache__/' \
    --exclude='*.tmp' \
    --exclude='*.pyc' \
    --exclude='*.o' \
    "$SOURCE_DIR/" "$WORK_DIR/"

# Remove files with exactly 0600 permissions
echo "Removing files with 0600 permissions..."
find "$WORK_DIR" -type f -perm 0600 -delete 2>/dev/null || true

# Function to check if file is text
is_text_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local mime_type
    
    # Check by extension first
    case "$file" in
        *.txt|*.md|*.py|*.sh|*.c|*.h|*.js|*.css|*.html|*.xml|*.json|*.yaml|*.yml)
            return 0
            ;;
    esac
    
    # Check by mime type
    mime_type=$(file --mime-type -b "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    case "$mime_type" in
        text/*)
            return 0
            ;;
        *)
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
}

# Function to normalize line endings
normalize_line_endings() {
    local file="$1"
    
    # Check if file has mixed content (binary data)
    if grep -qP '[\x00-\x08\x0B\x0C\x0E-\x1F\x7F-\xFF]' "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        # File contains binary data, don't convert
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Convert CRLF to LF
    sed -i 's/\r$//' "$file"
}

# Function to check if file has shebang or execute permission
should_be_executable() {
    local file="$1"
    
    # Check if file starts with shebang
    if head -c2 "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^#!'; then
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check if file currently has execute permission
    if [[ -x "$file" ]]; then
        return 0
    fi
    
    return 1
}

echo "Normalizing files..."
# Process all files
find "$WORK_DIR" -type f | while IFS= read -r file; do
    # Normalize line endings for text files
    if is_text_file "$file"; then
        normalize_line_endings "$file"
    fi
    
    # Set permissions
    if should_be_executable "$file"; then
        chmod 0755 "$file"
    else
        chmod 0644 "$file"
    fi
    
    # Set timestamp
    touch -d "@$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH" "$file"
    
    # Set owner/group
    chown 0:0 "$file"
done

# Process directories
find "$WORK_DIR" -type d | while IFS= read -r dir; do
    # Set directory permissions
    chmod 0755 "$dir"
    
    # Set timestamp
    touch -d "@$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH" "$dir"
    
    # Set owner/group
    chown 0:0 "$dir"
done

# Process symlinks
find "$WORK_DIR" -type l | while IFS= read -r link; do
    # Set timestamp (use -h to not follow symlink)
    touch -h -d "@$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH" "$link" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Set owner/group (use -h to not follow symlink)
    chown -h 0:0 "$link" 2>/dev/null || true
done

echo "Creating deterministic tar archive..."
# Create tar archive with deterministic options
# Use LC_ALL=C for locale-independent sorting
cd "$WORK_DIR"
export LC_ALL=C
find . -print0 | sort -z | tar \
    --format=gnu \
    --numeric-owner \
    --owner=0 \
    --group=0 \
    --mtime="@$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH" \
    --no-recursion \
    --null \
    --files-from=- \
    -cf "$TEMP_DIR/release.tar"

echo "Compressing with zstd..."
# Compress deterministically
zstd -19 --long --no-check --single-thread \
    "$TEMP_DIR/release.tar" \
    -o "$OUTPUT_ARCHIVE"

echo "Archive created: $OUTPUT_ARCHIVE"
echo "SHA256: $(sha256sum "$OUTPUT_ARCHIVE" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
EOF
",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ]
}

action_ref.content:
{
  "analysis": "The build script failed because rsync is not available. I need to modify the script to use standard tools like cp and find instead of rsync for copying files with exclusions.",
  "plan": "I'll update the build.sh script to use find and cp instead of rsync for copying files while applying exclusion rules. This will make it work with basic Unix tools that are more commonly available.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > build.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Deterministic build script for reproducible archives

SOURCE_DIR="/app/src"
OUTPUT_ARCHIVE="/app/release.tar.zst"
TEMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
WORK_DIR="$TEMP_DIR/work"

# Set default SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH if not set
SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH="${SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH:-946684800}"
export SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH

cleanup() {
    rm -rf "$TEMP_DIR"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

echo "Creating reproducible archive from $SOURCE_DIR"
echo "Using SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH: $SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH"

# Create work directory
mkdir -p "$WORK_DIR"

# Function to check if path should be excluded
should_exclude() {
    local path="$1"
    local basename="$(basename "$path")"
    
    # Exclude patterns
    case "$path" in
        */.git|*/.git/*) return 0 ;;
        */node_modules|*/node_modules/*) return 0 ;;
        */tmp|*/tmp/*) return 0 ;;
        */.cache|*/.cache/*) return 0 ;;
        */__pycache__|*/__pycache__/*) return 0 ;;
    esac
    
    # Exclude file extensions
    case "$basename" in
        *.tmp|*.pyc|*.o) return 0 ;;
    esac
    
    return 1
}

# Copy source tree with exclusions
echo "Copying source files with exclusions..."
cd "$SOURCE_DIR"
find . -type d | while IFS= read -r dir; do
    if ! should_exclude "$dir"; then
        mkdir -p "$WORK_DIR/$dir"
    fi
done

find . -type f | while IFS= read -r file; do
    if ! should_exclude "$file"; then
        cp -p "$file" "$WORK_DIR/$file"
    fi
done

find . -type l | while IFS= read -r link; do
    if ! should_exclude "$link"; then
        cp -P "$link" "$WORK_DIR/$link"
    fi
done

# Remove files with exactly 0600 permissions
echo "Removing files with 0600 permissions..."
find "$WORK_DIR" -type f -perm 0600 -delete 2>/dev/null || true

# Function to check if file is text
is_text_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local mime_type
    
    # Check by extension first
    case "$file" in
        *.txt|*.md|*.py|*.sh|*.c|*.h|*.js|*.css|*.html|*.xml|*.json|*.yaml|*.yml)
            return 0
            ;;
    esac
    
    # Check by mime type
    mime_type=$(file --mime-type -b "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    case "$mime_type" in
        text/*)
            return 0
            ;;
        *)
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
}

# Function to normalize line endings
normalize_line_endings() {
    local file="$1"
    
    # Check if file has mixed content (binary data)
    if grep -qP '[\x00-\x08\x0B\x0C\x0E-\x1F\x7F-\xFF]' "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        # File contains binary data, don't convert
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Convert CRLF to LF
    sed -i 's/\r$//' "$file"
}

# Function to check if file has shebang or execute permission
should_be_executable() {
    local file="$1"
    
    # Check if file starts with shebang
    if head -c2 "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^#!'; then
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check if file currently has execute permission
    if [[ -x "$file" ]]; then
        return 0
    fi
    
    return 1
}

echo "Normalizing files..."
# Process all files
find "$WORK_DIR" -type f | while IFS= read -r file; do
    # Normalize line endings for text files
    if is_text_file "$file"; then
        normalize_line_endings "$file"
    fi
    
    # Set permissions
    if should_be_executable "$file"; then
        chmod 0755 "$file"
    else
        chmod 0644 "$file"
    fi
    
    # Set timestamp
    touch -d "@$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH" "$file"
    
    # Set owner/group
    chown 0:0 "$file"
done

# Process directories
find "$WORK_DIR" -type d | while IFS= read -r dir; do
    # Set directory permissions
    chmod 0755 "$dir"
    
    # Set timestamp
    touch -d "@$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH" "$dir"
    
    # Set owner/group
    chown 0:0 "$dir"
done

# Process symlinks
find "$WORK_DIR" -type l | while IFS= read -r link; do
    # Set timestamp (use -h to not follow symlink)
    touch -h -d "@$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH" "$link" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Set owner/group (use -h to not follow symlink)
    chown -h 0:0 "$link" 2>/dev/null || true
done

echo "Creating deterministic tar archive..."
# Create tar archive with deterministic options
# Use LC_ALL=C for locale-independent sorting
cd "$WORK_DIR"
export LC_ALL=C
find . -print0 | sort -z | tar \
    --format=gnu \
    --numeric-owner \
    --owner=0 \
    --group=0 \
    --mtime="@$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH" \
    --no-recursion \
    --null \
    --files-from=- \
    -cf "$TEMP_DIR/release.tar"

echo "Compressing with zstd..."
# Compress deterministically
zstd -19 --long --no-check --single-thread \
    "$TEMP_DIR/release.tar" \
    -o "$OUTPUT_ARCHIVE"

echo "Archive created: $OUTPUT_ARCHIVE"
echo "SHA256: $(sha256sum "$OUTPUT_ARCHIVE" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
EOF
",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ]
}

>     fi
>
>     # Check if file currently has execute permission
>     if [[ -x "$file" ]]; then
>         return 0
>     fi
>
>     return 1
> }
>
> echo "Normalizing files..."
> # Process all files
> find "$WORK_DIR" -type f | while IFS= read -r file; do
>     # Normalize line endings for text files
>     if is_text_file "$file"; then
>         normalize_line_endings "$file"
>     fi
>
>     # Set permissions
>     if should_be_executable "$file"; then
>         chmod 0755 "$file"
>     else
>         chmod 0644 "$file"
>     fi
>
>     # Set timestamp
>     touch -d "@$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH" "$file"
>
>     # Set owner/group
>     chown 0:0 "$file"
> done
>
> # Process directories
> find "$WORK_DIR" -type d | while IFS= read -r dir; do
>     # Set directory permissions
>     chmod 0755 "$dir"
>
>     # Set timestamp
>     touch -d "@$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH" "$dir"
>
>     # Set owner/group
>     chown 0:0 "$dir"
> done
>
> # Process symlinks
> find "$WORK_DIR" -type l | while IFS= read -r link; do
>     # Set timestamp (use -h to not follow symlink)
>     touch -h -d "@$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH" "$link" 2>/dev/null || true
>
>     # Set owner/group (use -h to not follow symlink)
>     chown -h 0:0 "$link" 2>/dev/null || true
> done
>
> echo "Creating deterministic tar archive..."
> # Create tar archive with deterministic options
> # Use LC_ALL=C for locale-independent sorting
> cd "$WORK_DIR"
> export LC_ALL=C
> find . -print0 | sort -z | tar \
>     --format=gnu \
>     --numeric-owner \
>     --owner=0 \
>     --group=0 \
>     --mtime="@$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH" \
>     --no-recursion \
>     --null \
>     --files-from=- \
>     -cf "$TEMP_DIR/release.tar"
>
> echo "Compressing with zstd..."
> # Compress deterministically
> zstd -19 --long --no-check --single-thread \
>     "$TEMP_DIR/release.tar" \
>     -o "$OUTPUT_ARCHIVE"
>
> echo "Archive created: $OUTPUT_ARCHIVE"
> echo "SHA256: $(sha256sum "$OUTPUT_ARCHIVE" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
> EOF

{
  "analysis": "Perfect! The build script has been successfully created and tested. The archive contents show that all requirements are met: proper permissions (0755 for directories and executable files, 0644 for regular files), deterministic timestamps (2001-09-09 01:46 corresponding to SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1000000000), owner/group set to 0/0, and 46 files included after proper exclusions.",
  "plan": "The task is complete. I'll do one final verification to show that the build script is properly executable and provide a summary of the successful implementation.",

echo "Created /app/build.sh with the following features:"

echo "• Permission normalization (0755 for dirs/executables, 0644 for files)"

echo "• Locale-independent sorting"

echo "• Same SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH → Same SHA256 hash"
echo "• Different SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH → Different but deterministic SHA256 hash"

    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}
